message:
feat(tools,cli): expose list_agents MCP tool and pin service install paths

Add list_agents as a new MCP tool (#118) in the Identity cluster that
returns agent name, program, model, task description, inception_ts,
last_active_ts, and contact_policy for all agents in a project. Includes
TOOL_CLUSTER_MAP, metrics TOOL_META, tool directory entry, and server
registration.

Fix service install (#44) to pin absolute paths in generated systemd and
launchd units. The generated units now include WorkingDirectory and
Environment directives for DATABASE_URL and STORAGE_ROOT, resolved from
the current runtime config. Relative database URLs (the default
sqlite+aiosqlite:///./storage.sqlite3) are converted to absolute paths.

Both features include tests:
- list_agents tool: registers agents and verifies tool output
- service install: unit content tests, database URL resolution tests,
  launchd plist absolute path tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"br-97gc6.3.1","title":"Blockers: diagnose br doctor WAL/SHM anomaly and preserved recovery artifacts","description":"Investigate the current br doctor warnings about preserved recovery artifacts and a WAL sidecar without matching SHM. Determine whether this is stale debris, a real consistency risk, or a benign leftover; record a safe remediation path without destructive cleanup.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:06:42.249742525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T17:47:01.512425476Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","db","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:06:42.249742525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1260,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Evidence on 2026-03-18: the live anomaly is specific to .beads/beads.db-wal existing without .beads/beads.db-shm. After the long-running br writer exited, lsof showed no remaining open handles on beads.db or beads.db-wal, but the WAL file still persisted. Preserved recovery artifacts are separate from that live complaint: .beads/.br_recovery contains beads.db.20260313_224031_514673114.bak and beads.db-wal.orphan_20260313T224229Z, while the repo root also contains historical beads.db.bad_*, beads.db.rebuild_*, vacuum/reindex test files, and recovery_20260313T224002Z.","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:44:48Z"},{"id":1265,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"TopazIbis","text":"Evidence on 2026-03-19: `am macros start-session` initially failed in `/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust` because repo-local `./storage.sqlite3` was malformed and had a stale `storage.sqlite3-wal` sidecar without a live repo-local holder. `lsof storage.sqlite3 storage.sqlite3-wal` in the repo showed no open handles. The unhealthy listener on port 8765 is a separate process (`/home/ubuntu/.local/bin/am`, pid 3943291, cwd `/home/ubuntu`) holding `/home/ubuntu/storage.sqlite3` and `/home/ubuntu/storage.sqlite3-wal`, so it is not the writer for the repo-local DB. Safe non-destructive remediation for the repo-local mailbox DB was: 1) verify no repo-local open handles, 2) run `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am doctor reconstruct --dry-run --json`, 3) run `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am doctor reconstruct --yes --json`. That quarantined the corrupt DB to `storage.sqlite3.corrupt-*` artifacts and rebuilt a fresh `./storage.sqlite3` from the Git mailbox archive, after which `am doctor check` reported the DB accessible and I could register as `TopazIbis`. Residual findings after reconstruct: archive/db parity still fails (`messages archive=1951 db=1292`), `schema_version` is `user_version = 0`, the archive contains duplicate canonical message ids and missing `project.json` files for some archived projects, and the repo now has preserved `storage.sqlite3*.corrupt-*` / salvage artifacts by design rather than active live-state debris. Separate but related tracker evidence: `.beads/beads.db-wal` still exists without `.beads/beads.db-shm`, `br doctor` still reports that anomaly, and `br update` for this bead persisted the status change to `in_progress` but returned `UNIQUE constraint failed: blocked_issues_cache.issue_id`, so Beads mutation reporting needs separate follow-up.","created_at":"2026-03-19T17:47:01Z"}]}

After the long-running br writer exited, lsof showed no remaining open handles on beads.db or beads.db-wal, but the WAL file still persisted. Preserved recovery artifacts are separate from that live complaint: .beads/.br_recovery contains beads.db.20260313_224031_514673114.bak and beads.db-wal.orphan_20260313T224229Z, while the repo root also contains historical beads.db.bad_*, beads.db.rebuild_*, vacuum/reindex test files, and recovery_20260313T224002Z.","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:44:48Z"},{"id":1265,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"TopazIbis","text":"Evidence on 2026-03-19: `am macros start-session` initially failed in `/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust` because repo-local `./storage.sqlite3` was malformed and had a stale `storage.sqlite3-wal` sidecar without a live repo-local holder. `lsof storage.sqlite3 storage.sqlite3-wal` in the repo showed no open handles. The unhealthy listener on port 8765 is a separate process (`/home/ubuntu/.local/bin/am`, pid 3943291, cwd `/home/ubuntu`) holding `/home/ubuntu/storage.sqlite3` and `/home/ubuntu/storage.sqlite3-wal`, so it is not the writer for the repo-local DB. Safe non-destructive remediation for the repo-local mailbox DB was: 1) verify no repo-local open handles, 2) run `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am doctor reconstruct --dry-run --json`, 3) run `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am doctor reconstruct --yes --json`. That quarantined the corrupt DB to `storage.sqlite3.corrupt-*` artifacts and rebuilt a fresh `./storage.sqlite3` from the Git mailbox archive, after which `am doctor check` reported the DB accessible and I could register as `TopazIbis`. Residual findings after reconstruct: archive/db parity still fails (`messages archive=1951 db=1292`), `schema_version` is `user_version = 0`, the archive contains duplicate canonical message ids and missing `project.json` files for some archived projects, and the repo now has preserved `storage.sqlite3*.corrupt-*` / salvage artifacts by design rather than active live-state debris. Separate but related tracker evidence: `.beads/beads.db-wal` still exists without `.beads/beads.db-shm`, `br doctor` still reports that anomaly, and `br update` for this bead persisted the status change to `in_progress` but returned `UNIQUE constraint failed: blocked_issues_cache.issue_id`, so Beads mutation reporting needs separate follow-up.","created_at":"2026-03-19T17:47:01Z"},{"id":1268,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"TopazIbis","text":"Patched `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs` to handle the critical mailbox split where `am mail inbox` could fail with `Resource temporarily busy` while robot reads still succeeded. The fix adds a minimal inbox-read schema probe, a best-effort direct SQLite fallback for mailbox reads, routes `mail inbox` through that fallback when the async pool/init path reports a transient busy error, and covers the read-path with new CLI tests. Remote verification is running through `rch`; one unrelated pre-existing async-closure lifetime issue in `get_project_record()` had to be flattened first so the crate could compile. Attempted to notify other agents via Agent Mail, but the live server on `127.0.0.1:8765` still rejects `TopazIbis` because it is pointed at a different mailbox DB than the repaired repo-local one.","created_at":"2026-03-19T18:10:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.3.1.1","title":"Tracker blocker: classify live beads DB sidecars versus preserved recovery artifacts","description":"Distinguish the currently relevant live database state from preserved historical rebuild or orphan artifacts under .beads. The output should identify which files are evidence, which are stale debris, and which ones br doctor is actually complaining about today.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:41:45.312405542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:44:53.880724262Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T23:44:53.880032217Z","close_reason":"Completed: distinguished the live beads.db WAL-sidecar anomaly from preserved historical recovery artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","blockers","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:41:45.312405542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.3.1.2","title":"Tracker blocker: define safe non-destructive remediation path for stale beads recovery debris","description":"After the artifact classification is done, document a safe remediation policy that does not require deleting anything during the audit. Include what can be ignored, what should be quarantined later with explicit approval, and what would justify escalation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:42:00.171611958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:47:04.888550523Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T23:47:04.887932135Z","close_reason":"Completed: documented safe non-destructive remediation policy for the tracker anomaly during the audit","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","blockers","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:42:00.171611958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1261,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Safe remediation policy for the current audit: do not delete any .beads artifacts manually; treat the WAL-without-SHM condition as non-blocking only while sqlite integrity, DB-vs-JSONL counts, and br doctor write_probe remain green; serialize br mutations to one writer at a time; if the anomaly worsens or counts diverge, stop tracker writes, preserve the current .beads state, and use br doctor --repair during a quiet window instead of ad hoc cleanup. Historical .bad/.rebuild/.br_recovery files require explicit human approval before any deletion or archival action.","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:46:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.3.2","title":"Blockers: reconcile discrepancy between br dep cycles and bv graph health","description":"br dep cycles reports cycles while bv --robot-triage reported has_cycles=false. This currently looks like a scope mismatch between historical all-issue cycle detection and bv triage on actionable/open graph state, not a live open-work cycle.","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:44:23Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-aazao","title":"Verification Coverage Program: real-path unit tests, E2E completeness, and forensic logging","description":"# Verification Coverage Program: Real-Path Unit Tests, E2E Completeness, and Forensic Logging

## Why
The repository already has a large amount of testing surface area, but it would be inaccurate to claim any of the following today:
- full unit coverage across the workspace
- zero reliance on mocks, stubs, fake binaries, fake repos, or synthetic fixtures
- complete end-to-end coverage across every critical workflow and failure mode
- uniformly excellent, information-dense logging and artifact capture across all E2E suites

Current evidence for those gaps is already visible in-tree:
- deterministic stub encoder coverage in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/tests/toon_integration.rs`
- stub embedder / stub engine coverage in search-core and DB search-bridge tests
- stubbed LLM completions in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/llm.rs`
- mock release artifacts in `tests/e2e/test_idempotency.sh`
- fake repo / fake lock / synthetic owner-state scenarios in storage tests
- wrapper-style E2E entrypoints where artifact richness and logging density are not guaranteed to be identical suite-to-suite

## Goal
Create a disciplined verification program that:
- maps every important module and workflow to concrete tests
- maximizes real-path coverage rather than simulated-only confidence
- explicitly documents any remaining sanctioned substitutes that cannot yet be removed
- standardizes E2E artifact capture, logging density, and rerun breadcrumbs
- installs CI gates so future contributors cannot quietly regress realism or coverage

## Deliverables
- repo-wide audit of test coverage, realism, and logging quality
- policy for what counts as acceptable test realism and when substitutes are allowed
- burn-down plan for fake-only critical-path tests
- granular unit-coverage closure across crate clusters
- granular E2E matrix closure across transport, workflow, failure, and performance paths
- forensic artifact and logging contract for all E2E suites
- CI/reporting/sign-off layer for maintaining the standard

## Acceptance Criteria
- a future maintainer can answer, with evidence rather than vibes, which critical paths are covered by real-path unit tests, which are covered by E2E, which still rely on sanctioned substitutes, and why
- no critical user-visible or operator-visible path is validated only by ad hoc stubs or thin happy-path E2E wrappers
- failures produce enough structured data to debug without blind reruns
- the resulting bead tree is self-contained enough that implementation can proceed without needing to reconstruct this reasoning from chat history

## Important Notes
This is a repo-wide verification hardening program, not a single test bead. The point is to turn broad but uneven coverage into trustworthy and explainable coverage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:02.708072106Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:02.708072106Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","e2e","logging","repo-wide","testing","verification"]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.1","title":"Verification Coverage 1.1: repo-wide audit of test realism, gaps, and ownership","description":"# Verification Coverage 1.1: Repo-Wide Audit of Test Realism, Gaps, and Ownership

## Why
Before adding more tests, the project needs an explicit inventory of what is already covered, how that coverage is achieved, and where confidence is artificially inflated by substitutes, wrappers, or fixture-only paths.

## Deliverables
- enumerate the workspace by crate, major module cluster, and critical workflow surface
- map each surface to existing unit, property, integration, conformance, and E2E coverage
- classify each test by realism grade:
  - real-path / real dependency
  - deterministic local fixture
  - sanctioned substitute
  - mock / fake / stub
  - untested
- identify which tests are merely contract or formatting tests versus true behavioral tests
- identify ownership / expected home for missing tests so the backlog is implementable
- produce a prioritized gap ledger that distinguishes critical-path gaps from cosmetic gaps

## Acceptance Criteria
- future beads can point back to this issue as the canonical inventory rather than rediscovering the current state
- the audit explicitly calls out present realism exceptions such as stub encoders, stub embedders, stub LLM completions, mock installer artifacts, and fake filesystem/repo state tests
- the audit is specific enough to drive per-subsystem implementation work without hand-wavy “add more tests” follow-ups

## Important Notes
This is not a paper exercise. The output must be actionable enough that later beads can close gaps mechanically.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:02.997462159Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:02.997462159Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","e2e","repo-wide","testing","unit-tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.1","depends_on_id":"br-aazao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:02.997462159Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.1.1","title":"Verification Coverage 1.1.1: inventory unit, integration, and conformance coverage by crate cluster","description":"# Verification Coverage 1.1.1: Inventory Unit, Integration, and Conformance Coverage by Crate Cluster

## Why
A usable audit starts with an explicit map of implementation surfaces to existing nearby tests. Without that map, the rest of the verification effort becomes intuition-driven and repetitive.

## Deliverables
- enumerate crates and major internal module clusters
- record nearby unit, integration, property, and conformance tests for each cluster
- separate direct behavioral tests from formatting/contract-only checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- later coverage-closure work can point to one inventory instead of rediscovering the current state
- every major crate cluster has an explicit current-state line item","notes":"Started after bv robot triage.  {"id":"br-aazao.10","title":"Verification Coverage 4.3: workflow and cross-component E2E matrix completion","description":"# Verification Coverage 4.3: Workflow and Cross-Component E2E Matrix Completion

## Why
Transport coverage is necessary but not sufficient. The system’s value comes from stateful workflows that cross crates and surfaces: project bootstrap, identity registration, messaging/threading, contacts, reservations, build slots, search, product bus, share/export, install/migrate, and multi-agent coordination.

## Deliverables
- define and close a workflow matrix for the major user/operator journeys
- ensure workflows cover both happy path and important edge/error paths
- ensure workflow suites capture real state transitions in DB + archive + MCP/API surfaces + CLI/robot where relevant
- explicitly cover cross-project and cross-surface consistency, not just single-command success

## Acceptance Criteria
- critical workflows are covered end-to-end as workflows, not only as isolated tool calls
- later regressions can be localized to a workflow family instead of rediscovering missing integration tests
- the suite set distinguishes transport/API parity from multi-step business workflow correctness

## Important Notes

+/// List all registered agents in a project.
+///
+/// # Parameters
+/// - `project_key`: Project slug or human key
+///
+/// # Returns
+/// Array of agent entries with name, role (program), project scope, registration time, last seen
+#[tool(
+    description = "List all registered agents in a project.\n\nReturns agent name, role (program), model, task description, registration time (inception_ts), and last seen (last_active_ts).\n\nParameters\n----------\nproject_key : str\n    Project slug or human key.\n\nReturns\n-------\nstr (JSON)\n    Array of agent objects with fields: name, program, model, task_description, inception_ts, last_active_ts, contact_policy."
+)]
+pub async fn list_agents(ctx: &McpContext, project_key: String) -> McpResult<String> {
+    let pool = get_db_pool()?;
+    let project = resolve_project(ctx, &pool, &project_key).await?;
+    let project_id = project.id.unwrap_or(0);
+
+    let agents = db_outcome_to_mcp_result(
+        mcp_agent_mail_db::queries::list_agents(ctx.cx(), &pool, project_id).await,
+    )?;
+
+    let entries: Vec<serde_json::Value> = agents
+        .into_iter()
+        .map(|a| {
+            json!({
+                "name": a.name,
+                "program": a.program,
+                "model": a.model,
+                "task_description": a.task_description,
+                "inception_ts": micros_to_iso(a.inception_ts),
+                "last_active_ts": micros_to_iso(a.last_active_ts),
+                "contact_policy": a.contact_policy,
+            })
+        })
+        .collect();
+
+    serde_json::to_string(&entries)
+        .map_err(|e| McpError::internal_error(format!("JSON serialization error: {e}")))
+}
+

+## Shell E2E Suite Inventory (`br-aazao.1.2`)
+
+### Entry Surface
+
+- The authoritative runner is `am e2e run --project <repo> [suite...]`, with suite discovery documented in [AGENTS.md](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/AGENTS.md) and implemented via the CLI.
+- [scripts/e2e_test.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_test.sh) is now a compatibility shim. By default it delegates to `am e2e run`; only `AM_E2E_FORCE_LEGACY=1` keeps the old in-script execution path alive.
+- The common shell artifact contract is rooted in [scripts/e2e_lib.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_lib.sh): `e2e_init_artifacts()` creates `diagnostics/`, `trace/`, `transcript/`, `logs/`, and `screenshots/`, while `e2e_summary()` writes `summary.json`, `meta.json`, `metrics.json`, repro files, forensic indexes, and a validated `bundle.json`.
+
+### Suite Topology
+
+| Lane | Count | Shape | Representative files | Artifact richness |
+|---|---:|---|---|---|
+| Thin wrapper suites | 22 | `tests/e2e/test_*.sh` delegates directly to a sibling `scripts/e2e_*.sh` implementation | [test_http.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_http.sh), [test_cli.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_cli.sh), [test_tui_a11y.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_tui_a11y.sh), [test_share.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_share.sh) | Wrapper itself is thin; all useful logging comes from the delegated script |
+| Direct common-harness suites | 102 | Suite sources [e2e_lib.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_lib.sh) and owns its assertions inline | [test_stdio.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_stdio.sh), [test_macros.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_macros.sh), [test_workflow_happy_path.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_workflow_happy_path.sh), [test_robot.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_robot.sh) | Rich default artifact set: trace, transcript, diagnostics, repro breadcrumbs, summary/meta/metrics, validated bundle manifest |
+| Search V3 specialized harness suites | 4 | Suite sources [e2e_search_v3_lib.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_search_v3_lib.sh), which extends the base harness | [test_search_v3_http.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_search_v3_http.sh), [test_search_v3_stdio.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_search_v3_stdio.sh), [test_search_v3_shadow_parity.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_search_v3_shadow_parity.sh) | Richer-than-base: adds per-case request/response capture, ranking diffs, index metadata, and Search V3 run manifests |
+| Optional-harness soak/stress wrapper | 1 | Suite can source `e2e_lib.sh` when present but still carries a standalone fallback | [test_soak_harness.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_soak_harness.sh) | Variable; richer when `e2e_lib.sh` is present, less uniform than the main harness lanes |
+
+### Wrapper vs Direct Notes
+
+- The wrapper lane is concentrated in legacy/compatibility-oriented suites where the canonical logic already lives under `scripts/`: archive, CLI, CI, console, dual-mode, HTTP, share, serve, and the heavier TUI flows.
+- The direct lane is now the dominant path. Most suites under [tests/e2e](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e) are not thin wrappers; they are full suites that source the common harness and own their assertions inline.
+- The Search V3 lane is intentionally separate. It still inherits the base harness, but it layers additional ranking/index diagnostics under `test_logs/search_v3/...`, so it should not be treated as just another generic `e2e_lib.sh` consumer.
+- The soak harness is the clearest outlier: it tries to reuse the common harness, but it is still designed to degrade gracefully if that harness is unavailable. That makes it useful operationally, but less uniform as a verification artifact producer.
+
+### Harness Richness and Artifact Gaps
+
+- The common harness is already materially richer than a “stdout + exit code” shell runner. It captures structured traces, environment snapshots with redaction, transcript summaries, repro breadcrumbs, fixture IDs, and a validated `bundle.json`.
+- The biggest artifact-contract gap is that the common harness does **not** emit the per-suite `manifest.json` requested by [br-aazao.8.1](br-aazao.8.1). Current artifacts are rich, but case-level manifest structure is still implicit across `summary.json`, `trace/events.jsonl`, `bundle.json`, and suite-specific sidecars.
+- Thin wrappers inherit richness from their delegated scripts, but the wrapper files themselves add almost no observability beyond the delegation comment and fallback invocation hints. That is acceptable for compatibility, but not a substitute for auditing the underlying script.
+- Several suites explicitly weaken artifact enforcement by tolerating summary failure on some paths with `e2e_summary || true`. Representative examples live in [scripts/e2e_http.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_http.sh), [scripts/e2e_console.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_console.sh), [scripts/e2e_share.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_share.sh), [scripts/e2e_tui_full_traversal.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_tui_full_traversal.sh), and [tests/e2e/test_http_streamable.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_http_streamable.sh).
+- One suite calls out a known harness weakness directly: [test_check_inbox.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_check_inbox.sh) documents that `e2e_summary` may fail because of a pre-existing bug in `e2e_write_server_log_stats`, then suppresses the failure. That is a real artifact-confidence gap, not just a stylistic nit.
+- Compatibility fallback breadcrumbs remain widespread in suite headers. That is helpful operationally, but it means the docs/comments still normalize the deprecated shim path almost everywhere even though `am e2e run` is the authoritative interface now.
+
 ## Realism Clues Already Visible
 
 - [toon_integration.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/tests/toon_integration.rs) explicitly uses deterministic stub encoders.
 - The verification epic already calls out stub embedder / stub engine lanes in search-related coverage.
 - The verification epic also calls out stubbed LLM completions, mock release artifacts, and fake repo/owner-state scenarios that still need explicit realism grading.
 - [guard_env_tests.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/tests/guard_env_tests.rs) and similar harnesses correctly use local synthetic fixtures for isolated policy checks; these should be treated as local-fixture confidence, not full real-path workflow proof.
 
+## Realism Grade Rubric (`br-aazao.1.3`)
+
+- `R0 Real-path`: production code paths exercised with the real runtime and durable state machinery. Local tempdirs and ephemeral DB files are fine if the test is still using the real engine rather than a substitute.
+- `R1 Deterministic local fixture`: real code over synthetic local files, fixture payloads, or controlled repo/archive layouts. Good for logic and migration confidence, weaker for external integration claims.
+- `R2 Sanctioned substitute`: an intentional offline stand-in that preserves a meaningful contract boundary, but is still not the real dependency. Good for repeatability, not enough for sign-off on the replaced dependency.
+- `R3 Mock / stub / fake lane`: explicit fake behavior or mocked external surface. Useful for branch coverage and control flow, but should be called out as realism debt when it sits on a critical path.
+- `R4 Thin / fragmented / unowned`: little direct coverage, or coverage only exists indirectly through lower layers. These surfaces need explicit ownership before confidence claims mean much.
+
+## Prioritized Realism And Ownership Ledger (`br-aazao.1.3`)
+
+| Surface | Current grade | Evidence | Priority | Expected owner / next bead |
+|---|---|---|---|---|
+| Real DB lifecycle and query orchestration | `R0 Real-path` | [atc_experience_lifecycle.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/atc_experience_lifecycle.rs) and [query_integration.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/query_integration.rs) explicitly exercise real SQLite, real migrations, real queries, and real FTS with no mocks | Critical-path confidence already present; preserve it | `br-aazao.5` should extend this style when closing remaining core/DB/storage/guard gaps |
+| Direct transport/workflow E2E suites using the common harness | `R0 Real-path` with observability debt | Most `tests/e2e/test_*.sh` suites invoke the real binaries and real local HTTP/stdio paths via [e2e_lib.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_lib.sh), but artifact structure is still uneven and wrapper suites hide delegated logic | High | `br-aazao.8.1`, `br-aazao.9`, `br-aazao.10`, and `br-aazao.11` own the remaining artifact and matrix closure work |
+| TOON encoder integration and golden capture | `R2 Sanctioned substitute` | [toon_integration.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/tests/toon_integration.rs) and [test_toon.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_toon.sh) intentionally rely on [toon_stub_encoder.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/toon_stub_encoder.sh) and [toon_stub_encoder_fail.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/toon_stub_encoder_fail.sh) instead of a real encoder install | High: user-visible formatting surface, but current proof is offline-contract only | `br-aazao.3` should replace or explicitly isolate this lane with real-path inputs |
+| Search semantic/embedder engine adapter tests | `R3 Mock / stub / fake lane` | Search-core and DB search modules use `StubEngine`, `StubLifecycle`, and `StubEmbedder` in files like [engine.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/engine.rs), [fs_bridge.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/fs_bridge.rs), [two_tier.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/two_tier.rs), and [updater.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/updater.rs) | High: search quality claims are realism-sensitive | `br-aazao.4` owns stub embedder / stub engine isolation or replacement |
+| LLM-assisted thread summarization E2E | `R3 Mock / stub / fake lane` | [test_llm.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_llm.sh) declares itself “stubbed, offline” and forces `MCP_AGENT_MAIL_LLM_STUB=1` for deterministic output | High: product surface exists, but current evidence is explicitly synthetic | `br-aazao.4` should separate sanctioned offline smoke from any real-model confidence claims |
+| Self-update / installer release-flow E2E | `R3 Mock / stub / fake lane` | [test_self_update.sh](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/tests/e2e/test_self_update.sh) uses mocked release endpoints, local HTTP, and tiny synthetic payloads; related install/fresh-install suites also build fake destinations and fake tool installations | High: install/update failures are operator-facing and recent regressions proved this path is fragile | `br-aazao.3` should own real-path release/install inputs and demote the mock lane to explicit substitute coverage |
+| Share / reconstruct / archive salvage tests with synthetic repo data | `R1 Deterministic local fixture` | Share, deploy, storage, and reconstruct coverage often uses fake encrypted blobs, fake archive trees, or fake lock ownership to drive recovery logic, which is appropriate for fault isolation but not equivalent to full live archive round-trips | Medium | `br-aazao.5` and `br-aazao.7` should preserve the fixture lanes but add more end-to-end archive/recovery proof where the workflow matters |
+| Binary entrypoints, WASM, and agent-detect | `R4 Thin / fragmented / unowned` | [crates/mcp-agent-mail/src/main.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail/src/main.rs), [crates/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/src/lib.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/src/lib.rs), and [crates/mcp-agent-mail-agent-detect/src/lib.rs](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-agent-detect/src/lib.rs) have notably lighter direct harness coverage than the mainline crates | Medium, but easy to forget | `br-aazao.7` is the obvious home for WASM/agent-detect closure; entrypoint/runtime surface follow-up should also feed `br-aazao.6` where CLI/server behavior is shared |
+
+### Critical-Path Gaps To Treat As Realism Debt, Not “More Tests”
+
+- `TOON`: current coverage proves the envelope contract, not a real encoder deployment. This is a release-confidence gap, not a missing-assertion nit.
+- `Search + embedder + LLM`: these lanes already have good algorithm and contract coverage, but the substitutes are sitting on product-critical ranking and summarization paths. They need explicit realism boundaries.
+- `Installer + self-update`: the mocked release/test-install lanes are useful, but recent live failures show they cannot be mistaken for production-proof coverage.
+- `Shell E2E artifacts`: the harness is rich, but until `manifest.json` and summary-failure enforcement are uniform, downstream forensic automation still has blind spots.
+
+### Secondary Gaps Where Ownership Matters More Than Raw Test Count
+
+- `share`, `wasm`, `agent-detect`, and the binary entrypoint surfaces are the clearest places where coverage depth is still thin enough to warrant dedicated closure beads.
+- Local-fixture tests in guard/storage/share are doing the right job for narrow policy/fault logic. The missing piece is not deleting those tests; it is pairing them with a few higher-level real-path proofs where they currently stand alone.
+
 ## Obvious Follow-On Targets
 
-- `br-aazao.1.2`: full shell/E2E suite inventory, harness richness, and artifact-gap ledger.
-- `br-aazao.1.3`: realism grading and ownership mapping, especially for TOON, search/embedder, LLM, installer, and fake repo lanes.
+- `br-aazao.8.1`: add the missing per-suite `manifest.json` contract so the current rich artifact set becomes uniformly machine-readable at the case level.
 - `br-aazao.5` / `6` / `7`: use the per-crate laggards above to drive concrete closure work instead of broad “add more tests” efforts.
